content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 1, 1993


James T. LaPorte
Assistant County Counsel
Offices of County Counsel
Kings County Government Center
Hanford, CA 93230

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-315

Dear Mr. LaPorte:

This is in response to your letter requesting assistance on
behalf of Kings County Supervisor Abel J. Meirelles regarding his
responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since your advice request
pertains to possible future decisions, we are treating your
request as one for informal assistance.2

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTIONS

1. May Supervisor Meirelles participate in a decision of the
Kings County Waste Management Authority, a joint powers authority
comprised of the county and three cities in the county, regarding
the termination of a contract between the authority and the
Chambers Development Company, Inc., where the supervisor has

__________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-93-315
Page 2


received more than $250 in contributions from a person
representing Western Waste, Inc. within the 12 months prior to the
decision?

     1a.  Does the return of the contribution on July 29, 1993,
permit Supervisor Meirelles to participate in the decision to
terminate the contract with Chambers Development Company, Inc.?

     2.  May Supervisor Meirelles participate in a decision of the
Kings County Waste Management Authority regarding the possible
awarding of the contract to Western Waste, Inc., where the
supervisor has received more than $250 in contributions from a
person representing Western Waste within the 12 months prior to
the decision?

CONCLUSIONS

     1.  Since Western Waste, Inc., and its agent provided more
than $250 in contributions to Supervisor Meirelles in the past 12
months, and since Western Waste, Inc., is a participant in the
proceeding to terminate the contract with Chambers Development
Company, Inc., the supervisor may not participate in the
proceeding.

     1a.  Since more than 30 days elapsed from the time that the
supervisor knew of the contribution and the proceeding involving
the termination of the contract with Chambers Development Company,
Inc., the supervisor is still required to abstain from
participating in the Chambers proceeding.

     2.  Once the 12 months set forth in Section 84308(c) from the
time the contributions were received has run, the supervisor will
no longer be required to disqualify himself from decisions
affecting Western Waste, Inc.  Additionally, since a portion of
the contribution was returned (bringing the amount below $250),
the supervisor will not have an interest in Western Waste, Inc.,
with respect to future new proceedings.

FACTS

     The Kings County Waste Management Authority (the "authority")
is a joint powers agency comprised of Kings County and three
cities within the county.  Supervisor Abel J. Meirelles is a
member of the Kings County Board of Supervisors and a county
representative on the authority.

     On September 9, 1992, the authority entered into a contract
with Chambers Development Co., Inc., (Chambers) pertaining to a
materials recovery facility.  At that time, Mr. Robert Dowd
participated in the proceeding as the representative of a
competing business entity, Western Waste Inc.

     Since the award of the contract in September 1992, Mr. Dowd
has made various public statements before the authority, to
authority staff, and to authority members arguing that the
Chambers contract should be terminated in favor of Western Waste.

Our File No. I-93-315
Page 3


You stated that the possibility of terminating the Chambers
contract has been an issue discussed by the authority monthly
since January 1993. You also stated that contract termination was
a focus of the discussions by the authority in executive sessions
and public sessions at its meetings on June 9th, June 30th, July
14th, July 28th and August 11th. Mr. Dowd spoke in favor of
termination of the Chambers contract during a public session at
the July 14th meeting.

On February 4, 1993, Supervisor Meirelles received a campaign
contribution from Mr. Dowd in the sum of $500.00. On July 29,
1993, Supervisor Meirelles returned $300.00.

ANALYSIS

In 1983, Section 84308 was added to the Act in order to
ensure that appointed members of boards or commissions would not
be biased by large campaign contributors or potential contributors
who might appear before them in a proceeding involving a license,
permit or entitlement for use. Section 84308 applies to all
appointed officers of any state agency or local government agency,
with the exception of the courts or any agency in the judicial
branch of government, the Legislature, the Board of Equalization,
and constitutional officers. (Section 84308(a)(3).)

Section 84308 imposes two requirements on officers subject to
the section. First, no officer of an agency may accept, solicit,³
or direct a contribution of more than $250, from any party,⁴ of
his or her agent, or from any participant,⁵ or his or her agent,
while a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use is pending before the agency and for three
months following the date a final decision is rendered in the
proceeding. (Section 84308(b).)


³ This prohibition applies regardless whether the officer
accepts, solicits, or directs the contribution for himself or
herself, or on behalf of any other officer, of on behalf of any
candidate for office or on behalf of any committee.

⁴ "Party" means any person who files an application for, or is
the subject of, a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use. (Section 84308(a)(1).)

⁵ "Participant" means any person who is not a party but who
actively supports or opposes a particular decision in a proceeding
involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use and who
has a financial interest in the decision. A person actively
supports or opposes a particular decision in a proceeding if he or
she lobbies in person the officers or employees of the agency,
testifies in person before the agency, or otherwise acts to
influence officers of the agency. (Section 84308(a)(2).)

Our File No. I-93-315
Page 4


     Further, if an officer has in fact accepted a contribution of
more than $250 during the last 12 months from a party or
participant in a proceeding involving a license, permit or other
entitlement for use pending before an agency, the officer must
disclose that fact on the record of the proceeding and must
disqualify himself or herself from participating.  (Section
84308(c).)

Chambers Development Company, Inc.

     1.  License, Permit, or Other Entitlement for Use

     "License, permit, or other entitlement for use" is defined to
include all business, professional, trade and land use licenses
and permits and all other entitlements for use, including all
entitlements for land use, all contracts (other than competitively
bid, labor, or personal employment contracts), and all franchises.
(Section 84308(a)(5).)

     Regulation 18438.2(a) provides that for purposes of Section
84308, a "proceeding involving a license, permit or other
entitlement for use" includes any proceeding to grant, deny,
revoke, restrict, or modify a license, permit or other entitlement
for use.  A proceeding involving a license, permit or other
entitlement for use is "pending before" an agency when the
proceeding has been commenced, or the issue has otherwise been
submitted to the jurisdiction of an agency for its determination.
(Regulation 18438.2(a).)

     According to your facts, on June 9, 1993, the authority began
discussing terminating the contract with Chambers in executive
session.  Pursuant to Regulation 18438.2(b), the proceeding
commenced at that time.  Thus, if the supervisor received more
than $250 in contributions from a party or a participant in the
proceeding, the supervisor would be required to disqualify himself
from the decision on the Chambers contract.

     2.  Participants

     Additionally, you stated that Western Waste, through
Mr. Dowd, was actively involved in the proceedings.  Western Waste
was not a party to the proceeding.  Section 84308(a)(1) defines a
"party" as any person who files an application for, or is the
subject of, a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use.  It appears that Chambers was the only party
to the proceeding.

     However, Section 84308(a)(2) defines a "participant" as any
person who is not a party but who actively supports or opposes a
particular decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit,
or other entitlement for use and who has a financial interest in
the decision.  A person actively supports or opposes a particular
decision in a proceeding if he or she lobbies in person the

Our File No. I-93-315
Page 5


officers or employees of the agency, testifies in person before
the agency, or otherwise acts to influence officers of the agency.
Regulation 18438.4 provides:

     (a) A person "lobbies in person" when he or
she communicates directly, either in person or in
writing, with an officer of an agency for the
purpose of influencing the decision in a
proceeding.

     (b) A person "testifies in person" when he or
she testifies or makes an oral statement before an
agency during a proceeding on a license, permit or
other entitlement for use for the purpose of
influencing the decision of the agency.

     (c) A person "otherwise acts to influence"
officers of an agency when he or she communicates
with an employee of the agency, or when his or her
agent lobbies in person, testifies in person or
otherwise communicates with officers or employees
of the agency, for the purpose of influencing the
officers' decision in a proceeding.

     You stated that Western Waste, through Mr. Dowd, has been in
frequent contact with the authority, authority staff, and members
arguing that the Chambers contract should be terminated in favor
of Western Waste. Mr. Dowd was acting on Western Waste's behalf
as its agent. Thus, Western Waste actively supported the
termination of the Chambers contract.

     In addition to active participation, Western Waste must also
have a financial interest in the decision for Section 84308 to
apply. In this case, if the Chambers contract is terminated,
Waste Waste will be eligible for the contract. Thus, it is
foreseeable that the termination will materially affect Western
Waste pursuant to Section 87103, and therefore, Western Waste
would be considered a "participant."

3.   Aggregation

     Regulation 18438.6 provides that a person "accepts" or
"receives" a contribution if that person's
own candidacy or own controlled committee. On February 4, 1993,
Supervisor Meirelles received a campaign contribution from
Mr. Dowd in the sum of $500.00.

     Regulation 18438.3(b) requires that in determining whether a
contribution of $250 or more has been made by a person or his or
her agent, contributions made by that person within the preceding
12 months must be aggregated with those made by his or her agent
within the preceding 12 months or the period of the agency
relationship, whichever is shorter. Regulation 18438.3(a) also
provides that a person is the "agent" of a party to, or a
participant in, a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other

Our File No. I-93-315
Page 6


entitlement for use only if he or she represents that person in
connection with the proceeding involving the license, permit, or
other entitlement for use.6

According to your facts, Mr. Dowd has represented Western
Waste before the authority since September 1992. It appears that
Mr. Dowd also was a representative of Western Waste on February 4,
1993, when the $500 contribution was made to the supervisor.
Thus, the $500 is cumulated with any contribution made by Western
Waste. Even if Western Waste made no contributions, the
aggregated amount is in excess of $250.

4. The Return of Contributions

Section 84308(c) provides that an official may participate in
the proceeding if he or she returns the contribution (or amount of
the contribution in excesses of $250) within 30 days from the time
he or she knows, or should have known, both of the following:

A. About the receipt of the contribution; and,

B. The proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use.

Regulation 18438.7 provides:

(a) An officer knows or has reason to know
that a person has a financial interest in the
decision in a proceeding if:

(1) The person is a party; or

(2) The person is a participant and
reveals facts in his or her written or oral
support or opposition before the agency which
make the person's financial interest apparent.

(b) An officer knows, or should have known,
about a proceeding pending before the agency if
either:


6 If an individual acting as an agent is also acting as an
employee or member of a law, architectural, engineering, or
consulting firm, or a similar entity or corporation, both the
entity or corporation and the individual are "agents."

Our File No. I-93-315
Page 7


     (1) The officer has received notice of
the license, permit or other entitlement
proceeding. Notice includes receipt of an
agenda or docket identifying the proceeding
and the party or other persons affected by
name; or

     (2) The officer has actual knowledge of
the proceeding.

     (c) An officer knows, or should have known
about a contribution if:

     (1) The contribution has been disclosed
by the party pursuant to Section 84308(d); or

     (2) The officer has actual knowledge of
the contribution.

     According to you facts, Supervisor Meirelles received the
campaign contribution from Mr. Dowd on February 4, 1993 and
disclosed the contribution on February 15, 1993. On July 29,
1993, Supervisor Meirelles returned $300.00.

     You also stated that it was common knowledge as early as
September 9, 1992, that Mr. Dowd represented Western Waste.
Further, the supervisor was aware of Western Waste's interest in
the Chambers proceeding on June 9th. Thus, any return of the
contribution needed to occur in early July.

     Since it was more than 30 days after the two conditions noted
in Section 84308(c) were met, the supervisor is still required to
abstain from participating in the Chambers proceeding despite
returning a portion of the contribution.

Future Decisions Affecting Western Waste

     Clearly, in February 1994, Supervisor Meirelles will no
longer have an interest in Western Waste by virtue of the
contribution since the 12 months set forth in Section 84308(c)
will have run.

     Additionally, with respect to future new proceedings
involving Western Waste, the return of the contribution will have
been in a timely fashion as set forth in Section 84308. Thus, the

Our File No. I-93-315
Page 8


supervisor is not prohibited from participating in new contracts
for which Western Waste has bid.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.7

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Wayne Ordos
                                        Executive Director

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  John W. Wallace
                                             Counsel, Legal Division




_________________________
7 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

QUESTION: 1.
May Supervisor Meirelles participate in a decision of the
Kings County Waste Management Authority,
a joint powers authority
comprised of the county and three cities in the county, regarding
the termination of a contract between the authority and the
Chambers Development Company, Inc., where the supervisor has
et seq.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)

(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1—93—315
received more than $250 in contributions from a person
representing Western Waste,
Inc. within the
12 months prior to the
decision?
la.
Does the return of the contribution on July 29,
1993,
permit Supervisor Meirelles to participate in the decision to
terminate the contract with Chambers Development Company,
Inc.?
2.
May Supervisor Meirelles participate in a decision of the
Kings County Waste Management Authority regarding the possible
awarding of the contract to Western Waste, Inc., where the
supervisor has received more than $250 in càntributions from a
person representing Western Waste within the 12 months prior to
the decision?

CONCLUSION: 1.
Since Western Waste, Inc.,
and its agent provided more
than $250 in contributions to Supervisor Meirelles in the past 12
months,
and since Western Waste, Inc., is a participant in the
proceeding to terminate the contract with Chambers Development
Company, Inc., the supervisor may not participate in the
proceeding.
la.
Since more than 30 days elapsed from the time that the
supervisor knew of the contribution and the proceeding involving
the termination of the contract with Chambers Development Company,
Inc., the supervisor is still required to abstain from
participating in the Chambers proceeding.
2.
Once the
12 months set forth in Section 84308(c)
from the
time the contributions were received has run, the supervisor will
no longer be required to disqualify himself from decisions
affecting Western Waste,
Inc.
Additionally, since a portion of
the contribution was returned (bringing the amount below $250),
the supervisor will not have an interest in Western Waste, Inc.,
with respect to future new proceedings.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission September 1, 1993 James T. LaPorte Assistant County Counsel Offices of County Counsel Kings County Government Center Hanford, CA 93230 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-93-315 Dear Mr. LaPorte: This is in response to your letter requesting assistance on behalf of Kings County Supervisor Abel J. Meirelles regarding his responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since your advice request pertains to possible future decisions, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2 Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) QUESTIONS 1. May Supervisor Meirelles participate in a decision of the Kings County Waste Management Authority, a joint powers authority comprised of the county and three cities in the county, regarding the termination of a contract between the authority and the Chambers Development Company, Inc., where the supervisor has __________________________ 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660 Our File No. I-93-315 Page 2 received more than $250 in contributions from a person representing Western Waste, Inc. within the 12 months prior to the decision? 1a. Does the return of the contribution on July 29, 1993, permit Supervisor Meirelles to participate in the decision to terminate the contract with Chambers Development Company, Inc.? 2. May Supervisor Meirelles participate in a decision of the Kings County Waste Management Authority regarding the possible awarding of the contract to Western Waste, Inc., where the supervisor has received more than $250 in contributions from a person representing Western Waste within the 12 months prior to the decision? CONCLUSIONS 1. Since Western Waste, Inc., and its agent provided more than $250 in contributions to Supervisor Meirelles in the past 12 months, and since Western Waste, Inc., is a participant in the proceeding to terminate the contract with Chambers Development Company, Inc., the supervisor may not participate in the proceeding. 1a. Since more than 30 days elapsed from the time that the supervisor knew of the contribution and the proceeding involving the termination of the contract with Chambers Development Company, Inc., the supervisor is still required to abstain from participating in the Chambers proceeding. 2. Once the 12 months set forth in Section 84308(c) from the time the contributions were received has run, the supervisor will no longer
